title: Pilosella acutifolia
taxonomy: Pilosella acutifolia is a member of the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants that were previously classified as the genus Hieracium. Pilosella acutifolia is further classified as a species within the genus, and its exact classification is Pilosella acutifolia (L.) F.W. Schultz & Sch.Bip. The species Pilosella acutifolia is native to Europe and Asia. Pilosella acutifolia is a type of dandelion-like plant with small yellow flowers.
uses: Pilosella acutifolia has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Pilosella acutifolia, also known as Sharp-leaved Hawk's-beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella acutifolia include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pilosella acutifolia is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in combination with other herbs to create supplements and remedies for digestive and immune system support.
